candidates.organization: University of Illinois Chicago Microphysics Laboratory

public_claims: UIC identifies the Microphysics Laboratory as the only U.S. university laboratory performing fundamental HgCdTe molecular-beam-epitaxy research.
The laboratory is described as emphasizing II-VI materials and infrared-sensing devices.
The laboratory is described as having pioneered HgCdTe MBE growth, characterization, and device fabrication.
Current public contact information is provided for the professor emeritus associated with the laboratory.

unresolved_hard_gates: two target composition levels and specimen count
geometry compatible with both optical modalities
candidates.contact_action: request a non-confidential capability and specimen-availability discussion

candidates.organization: University of Texas at Austin E-beam Lab

public_claims: The JEOL JXA-8200 EPMA has five wavelength-dispersive spectrometers and quantitative analysis software.
The facility describes precision to hundreds of ppm for multi-element analysis under suitable conditions.
Beam diameter can be varied from approximately 0.7 to 50 micrometers.
The laboratory welcomes users beyond UT Austin and publishes an external-academic electron-microprobe rate.
Assisted analysis is available at a higher rate.

state_alteration_risks: conductive grounding or carbon coating normally required
electron-beam surface temperature may exceed 100 to 200 C
unresolved_hard_gates: validated HgCdTe standards and matrix corrections
beam-induced Hg loss or composition drift
non-destructive compatibility with subsequent paired measurements
native data, calibration, and covariance release
candidates.contact_action: request an HgCdTe-specific analytical feasibility review before any specimen preparation

candidates.organization: University of Tennessee Knoxville Electromagnetic Properties Lab

public_claims: The facility offers PPMS Hall measurements down to 1.8 K and magnetic fields to 9 T.
A DynaCool system offers Hall measurements down to 1.8 K and fields to 14 T.
The facility is available to external partners and clients and publishes assisted external rates.

unresolved_hard_gates: sample-temperature uncertainty at 6 K and 300 K
candidates.contact_action: request an assisted HgCdTe van-der-Pauw/Hall feasibility review

public_claims: UIC reports a PPMS installed in 2025 with electrical-transport capability down to 1.8 K and fields to 14 T.
The instrument is described as supporting research at UIC and other institutions.

publicly_supported_capabilities: potential co-location with the HgCdTe material laboratory
unresolved_hard_gates: Hall or van-der-Pauw option publicly verified
candidates.contact_action: ask whether the installed transport options include audit-grade Hall measurements for HgCdTe

candidates.organization: Washington State University Institute of Materials Research

public_claims: The facility describes PAS as sensitive to vacancy and vacancy-related defects and defect complexes.
Its 0 to 70 keV beam can provide depth-resolved measurements from the surface to approximately 10 to 20 micrometers, density dependent.
The page reports experience with CdTe and CdZnTe among many materials.

unresolved_hard_gates: sensitivity to the target Hg-vacancy state range
6 K and 300 K state measurement or transfer model
candidates.contact_action: request an HgCdTe-specific vacancy-proxy and depth-sensitivity feasibility discussion

candidates.organization: North Carolina State University Positron User Facility

public_claims: The user facility offers positron-annihilation lifetime and Doppler-broadening spectroscopy.
The facility describes semiconductor and optoelectronic applications including vacancy complexes, native defects, and deep-level defect mapping.
An intense positron beam supports depth-dependent studies.

candidates.contact_action: request a comparative HgCdTe feasibility assessment if the WSU route is unavailable or non-quantitative

candidates.organization: National High Magnetic Field Laboratory DC Field Facility

public_claims: Bruker FTIR systems support infrared transmission and reflectance from 10 to 6000 cm-1 with resolution better than 0.03 cm-1.
The public configuration supports fields to 35 T or 17.5 T and temperatures of 4.2 to 8 K depending on magnet and probe.
Up to four samples can be loaded in one cool-down in the 17.5 T optical system.
The DC Field Facility states that magnets are open to scientists through a competitive proposal process.
The facility publishes proposal deadlines and user-program contacts.
New users submit a magnet-time proposal followed by experiment documentation after approval.
A FAIR data-management form is part of the proposal documentation.

publicly_supported_capabilities: potential single-facility coverage of both optical classes
unresolved_hard_gates: HgCdTe sample and Hg-material safety acceptance
300 K paired absorption and magneto-optical operation
same-area registration across zero-field and high-field measurements
native interferogram, calibration, residual, and covariance release
candidates.contact_action: request a technical pre-proposal discussion before the next listed proposal deadline

public_claims: Zenodo assigns a DOI to published uploads and provides versioning, restricted-access, usage statistics, and GitHub integration.
The service is operated by CERN and OpenAIRE.
Zenodo accepts arbitrary file formats and guarantees bit-level rather than functional preservation.
Published records receive DataCite DOIs.
Zenodo describes versioned data files, retained original content, multiple replicas, tape backup, and checksum-based fixity checks.

unresolved_hard_gates: raw-data rights and embargo policy for all partners
project-side SHA-256 manifest and independent verification
retention and withdrawal requirements in collaboration agreements
candidates.contact_action: select an accountable archive owner and define the project data-release policy before deposit

controlling_blockers: no quantified Hg-vacancy proxy transfer uncertainty
no documented 6 K and 300 K same-area optical workflow
no agreed native-data, covariance, rights, cost, or schedule commitments
